description: Use this API to print the results of PV analysis from the .pv results file This API routine was first introduced in release 30.0.0. pv_print(resfile, restype, nconting, contingarray, noutputs, outputsarray, interface, enforcescale, uselinestyles, numberlines, prtparams)
python_syntax: pv_print(resfile, restype, nconting, contingarray, noutputs, outputsarray, interface, enforcescale, uselinestyles, numberlines, prtparams)

parameters.name: contingarray
parameters.description: An array of NCONTING elements specifying an array of the numbers of specified contingencies. The base case is marked by 0. The size of the array is equal to NCONTING. (input).

parameters.name: outputsarray
parameters.description: An array of NOUTPUTS elements specifying an array of the numbers of specified outputs. The count starts from 0. The size of the array is equal to NOUTPUTS. (input).

parameters.name: interface
parameters.description: Number of specified interface. valid only in case of bus voltage against interface flow (restype = 4). (input).

parameters.name: enforcescale
parameters.description: Allows to print the results with the different scales. (input).

parameters.name: uselinestyles
parameters.description: Use (or not) different line styles for plotting. (input).

parameters.name: numberlines
parameters.description: Put the numbers (or not) on the graphs. (input).

parameters.name: prtparams
parameters.description: Print (or not) the set of parameters used for calculations. (input).

enforcescale.description: the scales will be not changed according with the results; this allows to print the results from different files in the same scales.
the maximum and minimum values for the used scales will be taken from the file of results, and the printing will be done according with these values.

uselinestyles.description: all the lines will be solid.
five different (standard) styles of lines will be used: Solid, Dash-Dash, Dot-Dot, Dash-Dot, Dash-Dot-Dot.

numberlines.description: each graph will have its number for better recognition.

prtparams.description: the set of parameters will be printed.
